mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative return frequency remains below 45% when the trigger activates, or the signal shows no statistical edge in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that lifts corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield a positive return frequency below 50% over 60 days, or interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase indicate capacity constraints and slowing economic momentum, which trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: Signal direction flips to positive during late-cycle phases, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains negative across multiple expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price index readings compress real equity valuations by eroding future cash flow purchasing power, generating negative forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Positive return frequency exceeds 60% when CPI accelerates past the threshold, or rank correlation turns positive over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Combined signal produces negative forward returns in over 55% of cases, or interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Surges in real personal consumption expenditures during a post-shock recovery phase reflect pent-up demand and rapid economic normalization, which propel positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Signal yields negative returns in over 50% of triggered instances during recovery phases, or predictive power disappears outside recessionary troughs.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the index of industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and stronger corporate earnings fundamentals, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 52% during production expansions.
